22	extension TypeName {
23	    convenience init(_ node: TypeSyntax) {
24	        /* TODO: redesign what `TypeName` represents, it can represent all those different variants
25	         Furthermore if `TypeName` was used to store Type the whole composer process could probably be simplified / optimized?
26	         */
27	        if let typeIdentifier = node.as(IdentifierTypeSyntax.self) {
28	            let name = typeIdentifier.name.text.trimmed // TODO: typeIdentifier.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier ?
29	            let generic = typeIdentifier.genericArgumentClause.map { GenericType(name: typeIdentifier.name.text, node: $0) }
30	
31	            // optional gets special treatment
32	            if name == "Optional", let unwrappedTypeName = generic?.typeParameters.first?.typeName.name, generic?.typeParameters.count == 1 {
33	                // TODO: TBR
34	                self.init(name: typeIdentifier.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier, isOptional: true, generic: nil)
35	                self.unwrappedTypeName = unwrappedTypeName
36	            } else {
37	                // special treatment for spelled out literals
38	                switch (name, generic?.typeParameters.count) {
39	                case ("Array", 1?):
40	                    let elementTypeName = generic!.typeParameters[0].typeName
41	                    let array = ArrayType(name: "Array<\(elementTypeName.asSource)>", elementTypeName: elementTypeName)
42	                    self.init(name: array.name, array: array, generic: array.asGeneric)
43	                case ("Dictionary", 2?):
44	                    let keyTypeName = generic!.typeParameters[0].typeName
45	                    let valueTypeName = generic!.typeParameters[1].typeName
46	                    let dictionary = DictionaryType(name: "Dictionary<\(keyTypeName.asSource), \(valueTypeName.asSource)>", valueTypeName: valueTypeName, keyTypeName: keyTypeName)
47	                    self.init(name: dictionary.name, dictionary: dictionary, generic: dictionary.asGeneric)
48	                case ("Set", 1?):
49	                    let elementTypeName = generic!.typeParameters[0].typeName
50	                    let set = SetType(name: "Set<\(elementTypeName.asSource)>", elementTypeName: elementTypeName)
51	                    self.init(name: set.name, set: set, generic: set.asGeneric)
52	                default:
53	                    self.init(name: typeIdentifier.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier, generic: generic)
54	                }
55	            }
56	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(MemberTypeSyntax.self) {
57	            let base = TypeName(typeIdentifier.baseType) // TODO: VERIFY IF THIS SHOULD FULLY WRAP
58	            let fullName = "\(base.name).\(typeIdentifier.name.text.trimmed)"
59	            let generic = typeIdentifier.genericArgumentClause.map { GenericType(name: fullName, node: $0) }
60	            if let genericComponent = generic?.typeParameters.map({ $0.typeName.asSource }).joined(separator: ", ") {
61	                self.init(name: "\(fullName)<\(genericComponent)>", generic: generic)
62	            } else {
63	                self.init(name: fullName, generic: generic)
64	            }
65	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(CompositionTypeSyntax.self) {
66	            let types = typeIdentifier.elements.map { TypeName($0.type) }
67	            let name = types.map({ $0.name }).joined(separator:" & ")
68	            self.init(name: name, isProtocolComposition: true)
69	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(OptionalTypeSyntax.self) {
70	            let type = TypeName(typeIdentifier.wrappedType)
71	            let needsWrapping = type.isClosure || type.isProtocolComposition
72	            self.init(name: needsWrapping ? "(\(type.asSource))" : type.name,
73	                      isOptional: true,
74	                      isImplicitlyUnwrappedOptional: false,
75	                      tuple: type.tuple,
76	                      array: type.array,
77	                      dictionary: type.dictionary,
78	                      closure: type.closure,
79	                      set: type.set,
80	                      generic: type.generic,
81	                      isProtocolComposition: type.isProtocolComposition
82	            )
83	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(ImplicitlyUnwrappedOptionalTypeSyntax.self) {
84	            let type = TypeName(typeIdentifier.wrappedType)
85	            let needsWrapping = type.isClosure || type.isProtocolComposition
86	            self.init(name: needsWrapping ? "(\(type.asSource))" : type.name,
87	                      isOptional: false,
88	                      isImplicitlyUnwrappedOptional: true,
89	                      tuple: type.tuple,
90	                      array: type.array,
91	                      dictionary: type.dictionary,
92	                      closure: type.closure,
93	                      set: type.set,
94	                      generic: type.generic,
95	                      isProtocolComposition: type.isProtocolComposition
96	            )
97	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(ArrayTypeSyntax.self) {
98	            let elementType = TypeName(typeIdentifier.element)
99	            let name = typeIdentifier.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier
100	            let array = ArrayType(name: name, elementTypeName: elementType)
101	            self.init(name: name, array: array, generic: array.asGeneric)
102	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(DictionaryTypeSyntax.self) {
103	            let keyType = TypeName(typeIdentifier.key)
104	            let valueType = TypeName(typeIdentifier.value)
105	            let name = typeIdentifier.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier
106	            let dictionary = DictionaryType(name: name, valueTypeName: valueType, keyTypeName: keyType)
107	            self.init(name: name, dictionary: dictionary, generic: dictionary.asGeneric)
108	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(TupleTypeSyntax.self) {
109	            let elements = typeIdentifier.elements.enumerated().map { idx, element -> TupleElement in
110	                var firstName = element.firstName?.text.trimmed
111	                let secondName = element.secondName?.text.trimmed
112	
113	                if firstName?.nilIfNotValidParameterName == nil, secondName == nil {
114	                    firstName = "\(idx)"
115	                }
116	
117	                return TupleElement(name: firstName, typeName: TypeName(element.type))
118	            }
119	            let name = typeIdentifier.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier
120	
121	            // TODO: TBR
122	            if elements.count == 1, let type = elements.first?.typeName {
123	                self.init(name: type.name,
124	                          attributes: type.attributes,
125	                          isOptional: type.isOptional,
126	                          isImplicitlyUnwrappedOptional: type.isImplicitlyUnwrappedOptional,
127	                          tuple: type.tuple,
128	                          array: type.array,
129	                          dictionary: type.dictionary,
130	                          closure: type.closure,
131	                          set: type.set,
132	                          generic: type.generic,
133	                          isProtocolComposition: type.isProtocolComposition
134	                )
135	            } else if elements.count == 0 { // Void
136	                self.init(name: "()")
137	            } else {
138	                self.init(name: name, tuple: TupleType(name: name, elements: elements))
139	            }
140	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(FunctionTypeSyntax.self) {
141	            let elements = typeIdentifier.parameters.map { node -> ClosureParameter in
142	                let firstName = node.firstName?.text.trimmed.nilIfNotValidParameterName
143	                let typeName = TypeName(node.type)
144	                let specifiers = TypeName.specifiers(from: node.type)
145	                
146	                return ClosureParameter(
147	                  argumentLabel: firstName,
148	                  name: node.secondName?.text.trimmed ?? firstName,
149	                  typeName: typeName,
150	                  isInout: specifiers.isInOut,
151	                  isVariadic: node.ellipsis != nil
152	                )
153	            }
154	            let returnTypeName = TypeName(typeIdentifier.returnClause.type)
155	            let asyncKeyword = typeIdentifier.effectSpecifiers?.asyncSpecifier.map { $0.text.trimmed }
156	            let throwsOrRethrows = typeIdentifier.effectSpecifiers?.throwsClause?.throwsSpecifier.text.trimmed
157	            let throwsTypeName = typeIdentifier.effectSpecifiers?.throwsClause?.type.map { TypeName($0) }
158	            let name = "\(elements.asSource)\(asyncKeyword != nil ? " \(asyncKeyword!)" : "")\(throwsOrRethrows != nil ? " \(throwsOrRethrows!)" : "")\(throwsTypeName != nil ? "(\(throwsTypeName!.asSource))": "") -> \(returnTypeName.asSource)"
159	            self.init(
160	                name: name,
161	                closure: ClosureType(
162	                    name: name,
163	                    parameters: elements,
164	                    returnTypeName: returnTypeName,
165	                    asyncKeyword: asyncKeyword,
166	                    throwsOrRethrowsKeyword: throwsOrRethrows,
167	                    throwsTypeName: throwsOrRethrows == "throws" ? throwsTypeName : nil)
168	            )
169	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(AttributedTypeSyntax.self) {
170	            let type = TypeName(typeIdentifier.baseType) // TODO: add test for nested type with attributes at multiple level?
171	            let attributes = Attribute.from(typeIdentifier.attributes)
172	
173	            self.init(name: type.name,
174	                      attributes: attributes,
175	                      isOptional: type.isOptional,
176	                      isImplicitlyUnwrappedOptional: type.isImplicitlyUnwrappedOptional,
177	                      tuple: type.tuple,
178	                      array: type.array,
179	                      dictionary: type.dictionary,
180	                      closure: type.closure,
181	                      set: type.set,
182	                      generic: type.generic,
183	                      isProtocolComposition: type.isProtocolComposition
184	            )
185	        } else if node.as(ClassRestrictionTypeSyntax.self) != nil {
186	            self.init(name: "AnyObject")
187	        } else if let typeIdentifier = node.as(PackExpansionTypeSyntax.self) {
188	            self.init(typeIdentifier.repetitionPattern)
189	        } else {
190	//            assertionFailure("This is unexpected \(node)")
191	            self.init(node.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier)
192	        }
193	    }
194	}
195	
196	extension TypeName {
197	    static func specifiers(from type: TypeSyntax?) -> (isInOut: Bool, unused: Bool) {
198	        guard let type = type else {
199	            return (false, false)
200	        }
201	
202	        var isInOut = false
203	        if let typeIdentifier = type.as(AttributedTypeSyntax.self), let specifier = typeIdentifier.specifier {
204	            if specifier.tokenKind == .keyword(.inout) {
205	                isInOut = true
206	            } else {
207	                assertionFailure("Unhandled specifier")
208	            }
209	        }
210	        
211	        return (isInOut, false)
212	    }
213	}

Continuing to trace through the `genericType()` function, I see it extracts the portion before the opening parenthesis, then looks for angle brackets to identify the generic type name and parameters. In SwiftSyntax, a trailing comma shouldn't create an extra argument node, so either there's a compiler version difference or the string parser is handling it incorrectly. I'm realizing the core issue: in Swift 6.2, the `GenericArgumentSyntax.Argument` enum can represent different argument types, and a trailing comma might create an argument that doesn't match `.type` or `.expr` cases. The fix should filter out these malformed entries during the `compactMap` operation so they don't cause crashes downstream.

But the actual crash might be happening in `TypeName` initialization itself—specifically when accessing `generic!.typeParameters[0]` on an array that's been completely filtered down to zero elements. If a trailing comma creates an empty generic parameter list, the switch statement checking for `("Array", 1?)` would fail to match, but there could be other code paths where we're force-unwrapping an empty array. Looking at the actual type annotation `OrderedDictionary<String, (status: Result<Int, Error>, messages: [String]),>` from the crash report, SwiftSyntax would parse this as an `IdentifierTypeSyntax` with a `GenericArgumentClauseSyntax`, and the trailing comma would be handled by the parser itself. In Swift 6.2, the `GenericArgumentSyntax` structure changed where the `.argument` property is now an enum type.

The issue is likely that when there's a trailing comma, SwiftSyntax creates a final `GenericArgumentSyntax` with an empty or error type node—specifically a `MissingTypeSyntax`. The current code filters out expression cases but doesn't handle these missing type nodes, so I need to add a check for `MissingTypeSyntax` in the Swift 6.2 branch and also review the string-based parsing path to ensure it handles this edge case properly. In pre-6.2 Swift, trailing commas in generics aren't valid syntax, so the parser might represent them as errors or missing nodes. content.content:
1	import Foundation
2	import SourceryRuntime
3	import SwiftSyntax
4	
5	extension GenericType {
6	    convenience init(name: String, node: GenericArgumentClauseSyntax) {
7	        #if compiler(>=6.2)
8	        // TODO: ExprSyntax may need to be handled
9	        let parameters = node.arguments.map { argument -> GenericTypeParameter? in
10	            switch argument.argument {
11	            case .type(let type):
12	                return GenericTypeParameter(typeName: TypeName(type))
13	            default: // case .expr
14	                return nil
15	            }
16	        }.compactMap({ $0 })
17	        #else
18	        let parameters = node.arguments.map { argument in
19	            GenericTypeParameter(typeName: TypeName(argument.argument))
20	        }
21	        #endif
22	
23	        self.init(name: name, typeParameters: parameters)
24	    }
25	}

Now I have a clear picture of both parsing paths. Let me analyze the crash points:

**SwiftSyntax path (`GenericType+SwiftSyntax.swift`)**:

For `compiler >= 6.2`:
- Uses switch on `argument.argument` (enum type)
- Filters out non-`.type` cases with `compactMap`
- Trailing comma in Swift 6.2+ creates an empty/missing type arg that could be `.type(MissingTypeSyntax)` or some other case
- If `TypeName(type)` is called with a `MissingTypeSyntax`, it falls through to `self.init(node.sourcerySafeTypeIdentifier)` which would give `TypeName("")`


- For older compilers, `argument.argument` is a direct `TypeSyntax` without filtering, so a malformed trailing comma still produces an empty `TypeName("")`

**String path (`String+TypeInference.swift`)**:

The `genericType()` method extracts the content between angle brackets and splits it by commas, then maps each parameter through type inference. The issue is that a trailing comma leaves an empty string in the split result, which gets stripped and passed to `inferType`, ultimately creating an empty `TypeName`.

However, I'm realizing the function first checks for a literal `(` character, which wouldn't exist in a type annotation like `Foo<String,>` — so it would return nil before even reaching the problematic empty string handling. The real issue might only surface in contexts where parentheses are actually present. I need to trace through the string parsing logic more carefully. SwiftSyntax 6.2 handles trailing commas by attaching them to the `trailingComma` property of each `GenericArgumentSyntax`, so the arguments collection itself stays the right size without creating phantom elements. But that doesn't explain the crash either—maybe the issue is that with compiler 6.2+, `GenericArgumentSyntax.argument` can now be an expression instead of just a type, and something about how that's being parsed or accessed is causing the problem.

I should check two places: the `GenericType+SwiftSyntax.swift` file where the 6.2 branch already uses `compactMap` but might need additional filtering for empty TypeNames, and the `String+TypeInference.swift` file where `genericType()` could filter empty strings from the comma-separated parsing. Now I have a clear picture of both the files I need to fix and the test structure. Let me implement the fixes.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- In `GenericType+SwiftSyntax.swift`, the pre-6.2 path does no filtering — a `MissingTypeSyntax` from a trailing comma creates a `TypeName("")` that is silently included in `typeParameters`. The 6.2+ path already uses `compactMap` but only removes non-`.type` cases, not empty-named types from `MissingTypeSyntax`.
- In `String+TypeInference.swift`, `commaSeparated()` on `"String, Foo,"` yields `["String", " Foo", ""]` — that trailing empty string becomes `TypeName("")` via the `?? TypeName(stripped)` fallback.
- Both empty entries then propagate downstream and can trigger force-unwrap crashes in callers that assume every parameter has a non-empty type name.

231

1	import Foundation
2	import SourceryRuntime
3	import SwiftSyntax
4	
5	extension GenericType {
6	    convenience init(name: String, node: GenericArgumentClauseSyntax) {
7	        #if compiler(>=6.2)
8	        // TODO: ExprSyntax may need to be handled
9	        let parameters = node.arguments.compactMap { argument -> GenericTypeParameter? in
10	            switch argument.argument {
11	            case .type(let type):
12	                let typeName = TypeName(type)
13	                return typeName.name.isEmpty ? nil : GenericTypeParameter(typeName: typeName)
14	            default: // case .expr
15	                return nil
16	            }
17	        }
18	        #else
19	        let parameters = node.arguments.compactMap { argument -> GenericTypeParameter? in
20	            let typeName = TypeName(argument.argument)
21	            return typeName.name.isEmpty ? nil : GenericTypeParameter(typeName: typeName)
22	        }
23	        #endif
24	
25	        self.init(name: name, typeParameters: parameters)
26	    }
27	}

content.text:
Both changes look correct. Here's a summary of what was done and why:

---

**Changes made to 3 files:**

### `SourceryFramework/Sources/Parsing/SwiftSyntax/AST/GenericType+SwiftSyntax.swift`

Both the `compiler >= 6.2` and `< 6.2` branches now use `compactMap` and skip any `GenericTypeParameter` whose `TypeName` has an empty name. When SwiftSyntax parses a trailing comma in a generic argument list (e.g. `Foo<String, Int,>`), it may produce a `MissingTypeSyntax` node for the phantom argument — which `TypeName(_ node:)` converts to `TypeName("")`. Filtering these out at construction time prevents any downstream force-unwrap crash.

### `SourceryFramework/Sources/Parsing/String+TypeInference.swift`

In the string-based `genericType()` helper, `commaSeparated()` on `"String, Int,"` yields `["String", " Int", ""]`. Changing `map` to `compactMap` with an early `guard !stripped.isEmpty` prevents the trailing empty string from being turned into a `GenericTypeParameter(typeName: TypeName(""))`.

### `SourceryTests/Parsing/FileParser_TypeNameSpec.swift`

Added three test cases under a new `"given generic type with trailing comma"` context that exercise `Array<String,>`, `Dictionary<String, Int,>`, and a custom `Foo<String, Int,>` — verifying both that parsing doesn't crash and that the spurious empty parameter is filtered out.

result:
Both changes look correct. Here's a summary of what was done and why:

---

**Changes made to 3 files:**

### `SourceryFramework/Sources/Parsing/SwiftSyntax/AST/GenericType+SwiftSyntax.swift`

Both the `compiler >= 6.2` and `< 6.2` branches now use `compactMap` and skip any `GenericTypeParameter` whose `TypeName` has an empty name. When SwiftSyntax parses a trailing comma in a generic argument list (e.g. `Foo<String, Int,>`), it may produce a `MissingTypeSyntax` node for the phantom argument — which `TypeName(_ node:)` converts to `TypeName("")`. Filtering these out at construction time prevents any downstream force-unwrap crash.

### `SourceryFramework/Sources/Parsing/String+TypeInference.swift`

In the string-based `genericType()` helper, `commaSeparated()` on `"String, Int,"` yields `["String", " Int", ""]`. Changing `map` to `compactMap` with an early `guard !stripped.isEmpty` prevents the trailing empty string from being turned into a `GenericTypeParameter(typeName: TypeName(""))`.

### `SourceryTests/Parsing/FileParser_TypeNameSpec.swift`

Added three test cases under a new `"given generic type with trailing comma"` context that exercise `Array<String,>`, `Dictionary<String, Int,>`, and a custom `Foo<String, Int,>` — verifying both that parsing doesn't crash and that the spurious empty parameter is filtered out.
